label: Self-organizing maps & competitive learning

node.label: Self-organizing maps & competitive learning
node.definition: A self-organizing map uses competitive learning to place prototype vectors on a low-dimensional lattice that approximately preserves neighborhood relationships.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Self-organizing maps & competitive learning is to connect Unsupervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
examples: substrate for Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Unsupervised learning, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: Self-organizing maps & competitive learning follows the learning setup expressed by Unsupervised learning.
Self-organizing maps & competitive learning consumes, emits, or organizes information using Embeddings & distributed representations.
Self-organizing maps & competitive learning follows the learning setup expressed by Perceptron, Hebbian, Oja & spike-timing plasticity.
Self-organizing maps & competitive learning rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Geometry, topology & symmetry.
